h2h_summary: New Zealand and Belgium have no recorded competitive meetings prior to this 2026 World Cup match, making it effectively a first-ever major encounter between the two nations.
wc_history_home: New Zealand (the All Whites) have appeared at the World Cup in 1982 and 2010, most memorably going unbeaten in 2010 with draws against Slovakia, Italy, and Paraguay — the only unbeaten team at that tournament.
wc_history_away: Belgium have participated in 15 World Cups and achieved their best-ever finish of third place at Russia 2018, where their Golden Generation featuring De Bruyne, Hazard, and Lukaku defeated England 2-0 in the third-place play-off.
group_context: Group G consists of Belgium, Egypt, Iran, and New Zealand, with Belgium heavily favoured to top the group; New Zealand face an uphill task against stronger opposition but a positive result against Belgium could give them a fighting chance of advancing as a third-place team.
strengths_home: Defensive organisation and resilience — capable of frustrating top-ranked opponents
Set-piece and aerial threat through Chris Wood, New Zealand's all-time leading scorer
Experience of World Cup pressure, with the 2010 squad's unbeaten run embedded in team culture
strengths_away: World-class attacking quality with De Bruyne, Doku, Lukaku, and Trossard
Excellent goalkeeper in Thibaut Courtois (two-time UEFA Champions League winner with Real Madrid)
Strong squad depth and form — on a 13-match unbeaten run heading into the tournament
team_style_home: New Zealand deploy a pragmatic 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 built on defensive solidity, aerial duels, and set-piece threat through Chris Wood, relying on organisation and resilience rather than possession-based attacking play.
team_style_away: Belgium under Rudi Garcia use a 4-2-3-1 emphasising possession, quick transitions after winning the ball, with Kevin De Bruyne as the creative engine and pacey wide attackers like Jeremy Doku exploiting space on the flanks.
recent_news: Belgium have included striker Romelu Lukaku in their squad for the World Cup, despite his injury-plagued season. New Zealand and Belgium are set to clash in a crucial Group G match at the 2026 World Cup.
context: Group G consists of Belgium, Egypt, Iran, and New Zealand, with Belgium heavily favoured to top the group; New Zealand face an uphill task against stronger opposition but a positive result against Belgium could give them a fighting chance of advancing as a third-place team.
